volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 2.2.303b - Section 2.3.389)

sections.label: Section 2.2.303b
sections.translation: Woe to me , that I ’ ve got nothing with which to break this fellow ’ s head .

sections.label: Section 2.2.305
sections.original: Tun cyathissare mihi soles , qui ante hunc diem
sections.translation: Are you in the habit of filling the cups for me , who , before this day , have never beheld Epidamnus , nor been there ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.306b
sections.translation: Do you deny it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.307
sections.translation: Upon my honor ,, I decidedly do deny it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.307b
sections.translation: Don ’ t you live in that house ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.308b
sections.original: Di illos homines , qui illic habitant , perduint .
sections.translation: May the Gods send to perdition those that live there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.309
sections.original: Insanit hic quidem , qui ipse male dicit sibi .
sections.translation: Surely , this fellow ’ s mad , who is thus uttering curses against his own self .

sections.label: Section 2.2.310
sections.translation: Do you hear , Menaechmus ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.310b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.310c
sections.translation: If you take my advice , that didrachm , which you just now promised to give me —

sections.label: Section 2.2.312
sections.original: nam tu quidem hercle certo non sanu'ssanus es satis ,
sections.translation: For , i ’ faith , you really for sure are not in your senses , Menaechmus , who are now uttering curses against your own self .

sections.label: Section 2.2.314_315
sections.original: iubeas , si sapias , porculum adferri tibi .
sections.translation: you would order , if you were wise , a pig to be procured with it for yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.316
sections.original: Eu hercle hominem multum , et odiosum mihi .
sections.translation: Alas ! By my faith , a very silly fellow , and an annoyance to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.317
sections.original: Solet iocari saepe mecum illoc modo .
sections.translation: He ’ s in the habit of often joking with me in this fashion . How very droll he is , when his wife isn ’ t present . How say you —?

sections.label: Section 2.2.319b
sections.translation: What do you mean , you rascal ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.319c
sections.translation: Has this that you see

sections.label: Section 2.2.320
sections.original: tribus vóbis opsonatumst , an opsono amplius ,
sections.translation: been provided in sufficient quantity for three persons , or am I to provide still more for yourself and the Parasite and the lady ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.321b
sections.translation: What ladies — what Parasites are you talking about ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.322b
sections.translation: What , you villain , urges you to be an annoyance to him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.323b
sections.translation: Pray what business have you with me ? I don ’ t know you ; I ’ m talking to this person , whom I do know .

sections.label: Section 2.2.325
sections.original: Non edepol tú homo sanus es , certo scio .
sections.translation: By my troth , you are not a person in his right senses , that I know for sure .

sections.label: Section 2.2.326
sections.original: Iam ergo haec madebunt faxo , nil morabitur .
sections.translation: I ’ ll have these things cooked directly ; there shall be no delay . Don ’ t you be going after this anywhere at a distance from the house . Do you want anything ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.328b
sections.translation: You to go to utter and extreme perdition .

sections.label: Section 2.2.329
sections.original: Ire hercle meliust te interim atque accumbere ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ twere better for you to go in-doors at once and take your place ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.330
sections.original: dum ego haec appono ad Volcani violentiam .
sections.translation: while I ’ m subjecting these things to the strength of the fire [*] . I ’ ll go in-doors now , and tell Erotium that you are standing here , that she may fetch you away hence , rather than you be standing here out of doors .

sections.label: Section 2.2.333
sections.original: Iamne abiit illic ? edepol haud mendacia
sections.translation: Is he gone then ? He is gone . By my faith , I find by experience that your words are not untrue .

sections.label: Section 2.2.334b
sections.translation: Do you only be on your guard ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.335
sections.original: nam istic meretricem credo habitare mulierem ,
sections.translation: for I do believe that some woman in the harlot line is living here , as , in fact . this madman said , who has just gone away from here .

sections.label: Section 2.2.337
sections.original: Sed miror qui ille noverit nomen meum .
sections.translation: But I wonder how he came to know my name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.338
sections.original: Minime hercle mirum . morem hunc meretrices habent :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ tis far from surprising : courtesans have this custom ; they send servant-boys and servant-girls down to the harbour ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.original: si quae peregrina navis in portum advenit ,
sections.translation: if any foreign ship comes into port , they enquire of what country it is , and what its name is ; after that , at once they set themselves to work , and fasten themselves upon him ; if they inveigle him , they send him home a ruined man . Now in this harbour there stands a piratical craft ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.345
sections.original: aps qua cavendum nobis sane censeo .
sections.translation: against which I really think that we must be on our guard .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346
sections.translation: I ’ troth , you really counsel aright .

sections.label: Section 2.2.346b
sections.translation: Then , in fine , shall I be sure that I ’ ve counselled aright , if you are rightly on your guard .

sections.label: Section 2.2.348
sections.original: Tace dúm parumper , nam concrepuit ostium :
sections.translation: Be silent for a moment , then ; for the door makes a noise . Let ’ s see who ’ s coming out from there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.349b
sections.translation: Meanwhile , I ’ ll lay this down . ( He puts down the wallet .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.350
sections.translation: Do you keep watch upon these things , if you please , you sailors [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.3.351
sections.original: Sine fóres sic , abi , nolo óperiri .
sections.translation: Leave the door ajar [*] thus ; begone . I don ’ t want it shut : prepare , attend , and provide within ; what is requisite , let it be done . Lay down the couches , burn the perfumes ; neatness ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.354_355
sections.translation: that is the charm for the minds of lovers . Our agreableness is for the lover ’ s loss , for our own gain . ( To herself .) But where is he whom the Cook said was in front of the house ? O , I see him there — one who is of service to me , and who profits me very much . And right willingly is such usage shown to him , as he deserves to be of especial importance in my house .

sections.label: Section 2.3.360
sections.translation: Now I ’ ll accost him ; I ’ ll address him of my own accord . ( To MENAECHMUS .) My dear life , it seems wonderful to me that you are standing here out of doors , for whom the door is wide open , more so than your own house , inasmuch as this house is at your service . Everything ’ s ready as you requested

sections.label: Section 2.3.365
sections.translation: and as you desired ; nor have you now any delay in-doors . The breakfast , as you ordered , is prepared here ; when you please , you may go and take your place .

sections.label: Section 2.3.369
sections.translation: To whom is this woman addressing herself ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.369b
sections.translation: Why , I ’ m talking to yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.369c
sections.translation: What business have I ever had with you , or have I now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.370b
sections.original: Quia pol te unum ex omnibus
sections.translation: Troth , inasmuch as Venus has willed that you singly above all I should exalt ; and that not without your deserving it . For , by my faith , you alone make me , by your kindnesses , to be thriving .

sections.label: Section 2.3.373
sections.original: Certo haec mulier aut insana aut ebria est , Messenio ,
sections.translation: For sure this woman is either mad or drunk , Messemo , that addresses me , a person whom she knows not in so familiar a way .

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.original: Dixin égo istaec hic solere fieri ? folia nunc cadunt ,
sections.translation: Didn ’ t I say that these things are in the habit of occurring here ? The leaves are falling now ; in comparison with this , if we shall be here for three days , the trees will be tumbling upon you . For to such a degree are all these Courtesans wheedlers out of one ’ s money . But only let me address her . Harkye , woman , I ’ m speaking to you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.378b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.379
sections.translation: Where have you yourself known this person ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.379b
sections.original: Ibidem ubi hic me iam diu ,
sections.translation: In that same place where he has known me for this long time ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.translation: in Epidamnus .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380b
sections.original: In Epidamno ? qui huc in hanc urbem pedem ,
sections.translation: In Epidamnus ? A man who , until this day , has never put a foot here inside of this city .

sections.label: Section 2.3.381b
sections.translation: Heyday ! You are making fun , my dear Menaechmus . But , prithee , why not go in ? There , it will be more suitable for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.383
sections.original: Haec quidem edepol recte appellat meo me mulier nomine .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , this woman really does address me rightly by my name . I wonder very much what ’ s the meaning of this business .

sections.label: Section 2.3.384b
sections.translation: That purse that you are carrying has been smelt out by her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385b
sections.original: Atque edepol tu me monuisti probe .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , and rightly have you put me in mind . Take it , then ; I ’ ll know now whether she loves myself or the purse most .

sections.label: Section 2.3.387
sections.translation: Let ’ s go in the house to breakfast .

sections.label: Section 2.3.387b
sections.translation: You invite me kindly ; so far , my thanks .

sections.label: Section 2.3.388
sections.original: Cur igitur me tibi iussisti coquere dudum prandium ?
sections.translation: Why then did you bid me a while since prepare a breakfast for you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.389
sections.translation: I , bid you prepare ?
